hub_subtitle: Inglês para a vida real — falar, entender e ter autonomia no idioma

lesson.menu_title: The Things I've Done -- Experiences of a Lifetime
lesson.menu_desc: Talking about what you have done, without saying when -- 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 5 -- The Things I've Done
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Ana Claudia Veraldi | Lesson 5 -- The Things I've Done
lesson.grammar_point: present perfect for experience (ever, never, been and gone)

listenings.text: I have never understood bucket lists, honestly. My sister has one written down, with about forty things on it, and every year she crosses off two and adds five more. I find that exhausting. I have done a few big things in my life. I have lived in three countries, I have learned to swim at thirty-four, which was humiliating and wonderful, and I have never once regretted saying yes to something frightening. But I have also never climbed a mountain, and I do not think I ever will, and I am completely at peace with that. The list is not the point. The saying yes is the point.

People always ask what the turning point was, as if there is one clean moment. For me there really was. I had been doing the same job for eleven years, and a friend asked me a very simple question at dinner. He said, if nothing changes, where are you in ten years? And I could see it. I could see the whole thing, exactly. I have never been so frightened by an ordinary question. I gave up that job four months later. I have made a lot of mistakes since then, but I have never gone back, and I have never wanted to.

extra_audio.text: First, Tom asks Ana whether she has ever done something completely out of character. Then Ana says she has never been very adventurous. Next, she describes the biggest change she has ever made. After that, Tom asks if she has ever regretted it. Finally, Ana says it was the turning point of her life.
